anchor.snippet: Using linear algebra , a projective space of dimension n is defined as the set of the vector lines
A projective space of dimension 1 is a projective line , and a projective space of dimension 2 is a projective plane .

annotations.label: Projective space as set of vector lines

anchor.snippet: So, a projective space of dimension n can be defined as the set of vector lines
This set can be the set of equivalence classes under the equivalence relation between vectors

annotations.label: Projective space as antipodal point pairs

anchor.snippet: A third equivalent definition is to define a projective space of dimension n as the set of pairs of antipodal points
Given a vector space V over a field K , the projective space P ( V ) is the set of equivalence classes
the projective coordinates of a point P are the coordinates on the basis of any element of the corresponding equivalence class
If K is the field of real or complex numbers, a projective space is called a real projective space or a complex projective space
The complex projective line is also called the Riemann sphere .

annotations.label: Linear subspace is a union of lines

anchor.snippet: Every linear subspace W of V is a union of lines.
A projective subspace is thus a projective space that is obtained by restricting to a linear subspace
There is exactly one projective line that passes through two different points of P ( V )
Every intersection of projective subspaces is a projective subspace.
This projective subspace is called the projective span of S , and S is a spanning set for it.
A set S of points is projectively independent if its span is not the span of any proper subset of S .
If S is a spanning set of a projective space P , then there is a subset of S that spans P and is projectively independent
A projective frame or projective basis is an ordered set of points in a projective space that allows defining coordinates.

projective geometry is the study of geometric properties that are invariant with respect to projective transformations
a homography is an isomorphism of projective spaces

annotations.label: Projective space is a topological space

anchor.snippet: A projective space is a topological space , as endowed with the quotient topology
This shows that a projective space is a manifold.
it results that projective spaces are analytic manifolds

annotations.label: CW complex structure of real projective space

anchor.snippet: Real projective spaces have a simple CW complex structure
the fundamental theorem of algebra asserts that a univariate square-free polynomial of degree n has exactly n complex roots
Bézout's theorem asserts that the intersection of two plane algebraic curves of respective degrees d and e consists of exactly de points
So a projective variety is the set of points in a projective space, whose homogeneous coordinates are common zeros of a set of homogeneous polynomials
Any affine variety can be completed , in a unique way, into a projective variety by adding its points at infinity

annotations.label: Image of projective variety is closed

anchor.snippet: the image of a projective variety under a morphism of algebraic varieties is closed for Zariski topology

annotations.label: Projective space is a projective variety

anchor.snippet: A projective space is itself a projective variety, being the set of zeros of the zero polynomial.

a projective space S can be defined axiomatically as a set P (the set of points), together with a set L of subsets of P
If the (projective) dimension is at least three then, by the Veblen–Young theorem , there is no difference.
If the six vertices of a hexagon lie alternately on two lines, the three points of intersection of pairs of opposite sides are collinear.
The three diagonal points of a complete quadrangle are never collinear.

The geometric dimension of the space is said to be n if that is the largest number
There is a bijective correspondence between projective spaces and geomodular lattices
Dimension 0 (no lines): The space is a single point.
Dimension 1 (exactly one line): All points lie on the unique line.

every projective space of dimension n ≥ 3 is isomorphic with a PG( n , K )
A finite projective space is a projective space where P is a finite set of points.

The smallest 3-dimensional projective space is PG(3, 2) , with 15 points, 35 lines and 15 planes.

any algebraic (not necessarily linear) automorphism must be linear
The groups PGL are called projective linear groups .
